metadata.description: 结构型模式: Structural design patterns focus on the composition of classes and objects. Here is an example using Python to illustrate the Adapter pattern, which is a structural design pattern: ```python from abc import ABC, abstractmethod # Target interface class Target(ABC): @abstractmethod def request(self) -> str: pass # Adaptee class class Adaptee: def specific_request(self) -> str: return ".eetpad" # Adapter class class Adapter(Target): def __init__(self, adaptee: Adaptee): self._adaptee = adaptee def request(self) -> str: return f"Adapter: {self._adaptee.specific_request()[::-1]}" # Client code if __name__ == "__main__": adaptee = Adaptee() adapter = Adapter(adaptee) print(adapter.request()) # Output: Adapter: deetpad ``` This example demonstrates how the `Adapter` class adapts the interface of `Adaptee` to match that of `Target`.
